behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Real failure would occur during query execution: filtering logic in the query is broken, or stock-status field is missing. Mocking the product catalog to always return in-stock items would hide failures in the actual query construction or filtering. Testing against a local test database with mixed in-stock/out-of-stock products catches real filtering bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Populate test database with 100 products (50 in-stock, 50 out-of-stock) across categories. Generate recommendations for a user with no category preferences. Verify that (a) all returned products have in_stock=true, (b) zero out-of-stock products appear in results, (c) out-of-stock products are excluded even if they have high relevance scores.

behaviors.description: User preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Requires loading user settings from a data store and verifying they're correctly applied during recommendation filtering. Mocking user preferences could hide bugs in how settings are queried, deserialized, or applied to the product filter. Real failure surfaces when the system fails to load or apply blocked categories from the actual settings store.
behaviors.test_description: Create test user and set blocked categories to ['furniture', 'appliances']. Load test catalog with products across 10 categories. Generate recommendations for that user. Query tracking store or result set and verify zero products from blocked categories appear, even if they match browsing patterns or popularity.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm ranks products matching user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Requires the system to query user browsing history from a store and verify the ranking algorithm correctly weights it. Mocking browsing data could hide bugs in how the history is queried, indexed, or applied to ranking weights. Real failure occurs when the system fails to load browsing history or applies incorrect weights.
behaviors.test_description: Create test user with 10+ views in 'electronics' category and <2 views in other categories. Load test catalog with 100 products (30 electronics, 70 other). Generate recommendations. Verify that top-10 results include at least 5+ electronics products, showing browsing pattern influence. Repeat with different user to confirm pattern.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results with stable ordering—requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Mocking would always return deterministic results, hiding real non-determinism in database query ordering. Real failure occurs when the query lacks an ORDER BY clause or includes non-deterministic fields (e.g., insertion order, internal IDs without sort). Must test against actual database to detect ordering instability.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user, request page 2 with page_size=10 twice in immediate succession (no intervening changes). Capture full responses including product IDs, scores, and timestamps. Verify responses are byte-for-byte identical, including order. Repeat test 10 times against same database snapshot to ensure consistency.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Requires the system to query user's browsed categories and select diverse products from unvisited categories. Mocking browsing history or product catalog could hide bugs in the diversity constraint logic or category classification. Real failure occurs when diversity logic isn't applied during ranking.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with browsing history in exactly 3 categories (A, B, C). Load test catalog with 20 categories and 100 products distributed evenly. Generate recommendations (top 20 results). Verify that at least one product is from a category in {D, E, ..., T} (not in user's browsing history). Test with multiple users to ensure consistent serendipity.

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Requires the system to detect user has no history (query), fall back to popularity-based ranking (query popularity metrics), and execute that path. Mocking either the history query or popularity data could hide failures in the fallback logic or popularity ranking. Real failure occurs when fallback isn't triggered or popularity data is unavailable.
behaviors.test_description: Create new test user with zero purchase history and zero browsing activity (fresh account). Generate recommendations. Verify results are non-empty (>5 products). Extract popularity rank of each product from popularity store. Verify results are ordered by popularity. Confirm results differ from a power-user (who gets personalized recommendations).

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Real failure occurs when click data is written incorrectly to the tracking store (wrong product, wrong position, missing timestamp, or timestamp skew). Mocking the storage layer would always succeed, hiding write failures, schema mismatches, or data corruption. Must verify against actual tracking data store.
behaviors.test_description: Generate 10 recommendations for user, assign positions 0-9. Simulate clicks on products at positions 0, 3, 7. Query click tracking store (database table or log). For each click, verify (a) product_id matches the recommendation at that position, (b) position field matches the click position, (c) timestamp is within 1 second of click time. Verify no extra or missing click records.

behaviors.description: Response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Mocking the product catalog hides real performance issues (missing indexes, slow join queries, O(n²) algorithm). Real failure occurs during query execution against realistic-scale data. Must test against actual database with 100k products to expose N+1 queries, unindexed filters, or slow ranking algorithms. Achieving <200ms at scale requires integrating with database infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Populate test database with 100,000 products with realistic schema (id, category, stock_status, popularity_score, attributes). Create user with complex history (50+ browsing events, 10+ purchases). Generate recommendations 10 times. Measure wall-clock time for each generation. Verify mean response time < 200ms and 99th percentile < 500ms. Profile for N+1 queries and slow filtering.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Requires executing the recommendation pipeline twice (before and after a state change) and comparing rankings. Real failure occurs when the purchase signal isn't queried, isn't stored, or isn't applied to ranking weights. Mocking purchase history could hide failures in how purchases are persisted or applied.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user (capture top-5 product IDs and rankings). Simulate purchase of a product that appeared in top-5. Generate new recommendations for same user. Verify the purchased product either (a) does not appear in new top-10, or (b) ranks significantly lower than its original position. Repeat with 5 different users and products.

behaviors.description: Recommendation results are deterministic for same user state—requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns identical ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Mocking would always be deterministic, hiding real non-determinism in the actual system. Real failure occurs from non-deterministic database query ordering, random elements in the ranking algorithm, or cache inconsistencies. Must test against actual database and recommendation system to detect randomness or ordering instability.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user, capture full response (product IDs, scores, order). Wait 1 second (no intervening changes). Generate recommendations again. Verify responses are identical: same product IDs, same order, same scores. Repeat 20 times for same user to ensure consistency. Test with users having different history complexity.
